# Factorial ANOVA (between subjects) {#sec-ISACh9}

## Learning goals

### SPSS

1. Perform and interpret output from factorial ANOVA


## Main effects and interactions in factorial ANOVA

```{r}

library(ggplot2)

## good plotting stuff
# https://www.sthda.com/english/wiki/wiki.php?id_contents=7930
# https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/egg/vignettes/Ecosystem.html
# https://www.datanovia.com/en/blog/ggplot-title-subtitle-and-caption/
#   https://www.tidyverse.org/blog/2024/02/ggplot2-3-5-0-legends/
#   https://www.sthda.com/english/wiki/ggplot2-legend-easy-steps-to-change-the-position-and-the-appearance-of-a-graph-legend-in-r-software
# https://www.sthda.com/english/wiki/ggplot2-axis-ticks-a-guide-to-customize-tick-marks-and-labels
# https://www.sthda.com/english/wiki/ggplot2-line-plot-quick-start-guide-r-software-and-data-visualization

data_ME1<-tibble::tibble(Student=rep(c("Fine Art", "Politics"),each=2), 
                         Drink=rep(c("Beer", "Wine"),times=2), 
                         Score=c(11, 21, 9, 19))

data_ME2<-tibble::tibble(Student=rep(c("Fine Art", "Politics"),each=2), 
                         Drink=rep(c("Beer", "Wine"),times=2), 
                         Score=c(25, 25, 15, 15))

data_CrossInt<-tibble::tibble(Student=rep(c("Fine Art", "Politics"),each=2), 
                         Drink=rep(c("Beer", "Wine"),times=2), 
                         Score=c(15, 25, 25, 15))

data_ME_Int<-tibble::tibble(Student=rep(c("Fine Art", "Politics"),each=2), 
                         Drink=rep(c("Beer", "Wine"),times=2), 
                         Score=c(15, 25, 5, 5))


p1<-ggplot(data_ME1, aes(x=Drink, y=Score, group=Student)) +
  theme_bw()+
  geom_line(aes(color=Student))+
  geom_point(aes(color=Student))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("Blue","Black"))+
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0, 30),breaks=seq(0,30,5))+
  # theme(legend.position = c(.935, .9))+
  # theme(legend.position="top")+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())+
  ylab("Lang. score")+
  labs(subtitle = "Main effect: drink\n ")
  # theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust=0))

p2<-ggplot(data_ME2, aes(x=Drink, y=Score, group=Student)) +
  theme_bw()+
  geom_line(aes(color=Student))+
  geom_point(aes(color=Student))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("Blue","Black"))+
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0, 30),breaks=seq(0,30,5))+
  # theme(legend.position = c(.935, .9))+
  # theme(legend.position="none")+
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank())+
  labs(subtitle = "Main effect: major\n ")
  # theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust=0))

p3<-ggplot(data_CrossInt, aes(x=Drink, y=Score, group=Student)) +
  theme_bw()+
  geom_line(aes(color=Student))+
  geom_point(aes(color=Student))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("Blue","Black"))+
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0, 30),breaks=seq(0,30,5))+
  # theme(legend.position = c(.935, .9))+
  # theme(legend.position="none")+
  ylab("Lang. score")+
  labs(subtitle = "'Crossover' interaction effect\n(no main effects)")
  # theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust=0))

p4<-ggplot(data_ME_Int, aes(x=Drink, y=Score, group=Student)) +
  theme_bw()+
  geom_line(aes(color=Student))+
  geom_point(aes(color=Student))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("Blue","Black"))+
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0, 30),breaks=seq(0,30,5))+
  # theme(legend.position="none")+
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())+
  labs(subtitle = "Interaction (drink effect only for 'Fine Art')\n ")
  # theme(plot.caption = element_text(hjust=0))


ggpubr::ggarrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol=2, nrow=2, common.legend = TRUE, legend="bottom")

# gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol=2, nrow =2)



```

### Explainer video

[ANOVA, drugtrial]
